repository_conventions.summary: Do not use waitForTimeout. Use Playwright auto-waiting via locator assertions instead.

findings.title: Hard sleep waitForTimeout(2000) before payment section assertion

evidence.summary: waitForTimeout(2000) added before asserting paymentSection visibility

findings.why_it_matters: waitForTimeout causes the test to sleep for a fixed duration regardless of actual page state. If the payment section loads slower than 2 seconds in CI, the test fails. If it loads faster, time is wasted. This masks the real issue: the assertion should retry automatically using Playwright's built-in waiting.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove waitForTimeout(2000). The following toBeVisible() assertion already auto-waits: `await expect(checkoutPage.paymentSection).toBeVisible();`

findings.title: Hard sleep waitForTimeout(3000) before URL assertion

evidence.summary: waitForTimeout(3000) added before asserting confirmation page URL

findings.why_it_matters: Same issue as f-1. The URL assertion and heading assertion auto-wait — the fixed sleep is unnecessary and makes the test brittle on slow environments.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove waitForTimeout(3000). Use `await expect(page).toHaveURL('/confirmation');` directly — it retries until the URL matches or the timeout expires.

top_risks: tests/checkout.spec.ts introduces two waitForTimeout hard sleeps that will cause intermittent failures in slow CI environments and mask real page loading issues
